manifest.name: Focus Workbench

name: Focus Workbench

releases.author: PavelPeng7
releases.description:
## Focus Workbench 0.1.3

### Improvements

- Restricted vault access to the folders configured in Focus Workbench instead of enumerating every file in the vault.
- Added GitHub Artifact Attestations for release assets, so users can verify build provenance.
- Added `package-lock.json` for reproducible builds.
- Removed `!important` declarations to improve compatibility with Obsidian themes and user styles.

### Privacy

Focus Workbench remains fully local-first. It does not make network requests, collect telemetry, or upload vault content.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/PavelPeng7/obsidian-ultra-dashboard/compare/0.1.1...0.1.3
releases.name: Focus Workbench 0.1.3

releases.author: PavelPeng7
releases.description:
## Focus Workbench 0.1.1

Focus Workbench is a local-first workspace for managing tasks, focus time, ideas, and knowledge notes in Obsidian.

### What’s new

- Renamed the plugin from **Pavel Dashboard** to **Focus Workbench**.
- Updated the plugin ID to `focus-workbench`.
- Updated view, command, and settings labels to use the new name.
- Refined the plugin description for public release.

### Features

- Dashboard for focus tasks, idea capture, and knowledge notes.
- Task workbench with today, active, in-progress, overdue, and completed views.
- Markdown frontmatter-based task editing and completion.
- Local focus timer stored directly in task notes.
- Optional `.base` file support for task scoping.
- No network requests, telemetry, or external data upload.

### Privacy

Focus Workbench is local-first. Your notes and task data remain in your Obsidian vault.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/PavelPeng7/obsidian-ultra-dashboard/commits/0.1.1
releases.name: Focus Workbench 0.1.1
